name: Rune
description: An embeddable dynamic programming language for Rust with first-class async support and Rust-like syntax

language-family.comment: Designed to feel like 'Rust without types'

paradigm.comment: Supports imperative, functional (closures, iterators), and async programming

closures.comment: Async closures supported with variable capture

error-handling.comment: Rust-style Result types with try operator (?) support

async-await.comment: First-class async support with async functions, closures, blocks, generators, and select

standard-library.comment: Includes std::future, time, JSON support, and common utilities

execution-strategy.comment: Stack-based virtual machine with compact bytecode representation

compilation-model.comment: Can compile at runtime or precompile; supports const evaluation

repl-support.comment: Playground available; CLI tools for interactive use

debugging-support.comment: No dedicated debugging interface documented; basic error diagnostics available

source-maps.comment: Uses codespan-reporting for error diagnostics with source locations

startup-time.comment: Designed for quick script compilation; minimalistic stack-based runtime

execution-speed.comment: Goal is performance comparable to Lua and Python; eventually LuaJIT with Cranelift

memory-overhead.comment: Reference counting, no heavy GC; 5.5MB package size

api-safety.comment: Safe abstractions via Ref and Mut types; no unsafe required in user code

idiomatic-api.comment: Derive macros (#[derive(Any)], ToValue, FromValue), #[function] attribute, builder pattern for modules. 99.95% documented.

serde-integration.comment: Out-of-the-box serde support for vectors, objects, tuples; JSON module included

async-compatible.comment: First-class native async support built into the language

function-registration.comment: #[function] attribute macro and Module builder for registration

type-mapping.comment: ToValue and FromValue traits with derive macros for automatic conversion

no-std-compatible.comment: Dedicated no_std module with helper prelude and static_env! macro

blank-slate-mode.comment: Modules are opt-in; can create minimal context without standard modules

memory-sandboxing.comment: Custom allocator-aware collections in rune-alloc allow fallible allocations

cpu-limits.comment: Instruction budget system; playground limits to 1M instructions; const eval budget defaults to 1000

memory-limits.comment: rune-alloc provides memory-limited allocations that error instead of abort

syscall-restrictions.comment: No built-in file/network I/O; capabilities must be explicitly provided via modules

capability-security.comment: Module-based capability system; scripts only access installed modules

default-deny.comment: Scripts have no I/O capabilities by default; must explicitly install modules

share-data.comment: Ref and Mut types for safe references; also supports cloning via ToValue/FromValue

custom-types.comment: Structs and enums with associated functions via #[derive(Any)]

callbacks-events.comment: Functions and closures can be passed to Rust as callbacks

example-projects.comment: Used in 24 crates; game scripting and plugin systems

documentation-quality.comment: 99.95% documented API, book available, but some advanced topics less detailed

bus-factor.comment: 46 contributors; primary maintainer (udoprog) with active community

maturity.comment: v0.14.x, API still evolving, author notes compiler is 'in flux'
